column2: findings: Findings: •There is extensive vascular calcification, notably along the abdominal aorta, consistent with advanced atherosclerotic disease. •Evidence of grade I spondylolisthesis is noted at the L4–L5 level, with anterior displacement of L4 over L5. column2: findings: Right hemi sacralized L5 vertebral with rudimentary L5/S1 disc space. Internal fixation of D12 and L2 vertebral bodies using metallic plates and screws. Reduced height and anterior wedging of L1 vertebral body with no retropulsion. Mildly straightened lumbar lordosis denoting muscles spasm with mild scoliosis to left side. No evidence of spondylolisthesis. Small anterior osteophytes of D12 and L1 vertebral end plates. Maintained other disc spaces. No abnormal para spinal soft tissue shadows. No significant apophyseal joint arthrosis. Intact both sacroiliac joints. impressions: Mild lumbar spondylotic changes. Old L1 vertebral fractures with internal fixation of D12 and L2 by metallic plates and screws. For clinical assessment , comparison with old studies and follow up.
